---
title: "Research question"
format: docx
---

Modelling offers an interpretation when there is no single measureable or accessible source of truth. 
Interpretation implies judgement and therefore expert elicitation, in a similar process to a Bayesian workflow approach.
Uncertainty among what expertise is necessary suggests eliciting multiple judgements.
Faced with multiple judgements in turn requires comparability, and comparison.
Comparison requires criteria on which to compare.
Selection of criteria requires a meta-judgement about relevance.
Relevance requires prioritisation among outcomes.


What is the marginal value of information in emergency epidemic intelligence? Characterising the relationship between quality and quantity

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false

library(flextable)

# Create data frame with all body rows
df_main <- data.frame(
  Col1 = c("Quality", "", "Case study: Evolution of SPI-M"),
  Col2 = c("Evidence", "Advice", ""),
  Col3 = c(
    "Where are the gaps (low hanging fruit) in real-time public health data science? comparing best practice methods against sitreps: eg WHO DONs",
    "Which features of real-time intelligence are of highest value to public health? Discrete choice experiments",
    "What need was SPI-M established to serve? Documentary research & interviews; historical narrative situating SPI-M within political context"
  ),
  Col4 = c(
    "Which models are best suited to different epidemic intelligence tasks? Identifying baseline models from analysis of Hub forecasts",
    "How do group dynamics influence model comparison and consensus? Networks, bibliometrics, workshop/focus groups",
    "How accurate was SPI-M work during COVID-19? Evaluation of SPI-M consensus statements; Rt comparison"
  ),
  Col5 = c(
    "When does adding more modelling work become redundant? Model 'genealogy' among Hubs; analysis of variance of error",
    "What are the ethical responsibilities of modellers when influencing public health policy? Community of interest in evaluation and ethics",
    "What's the boundary between SPI-M and UK public health intelligence? Stakeholder mapping, identifying changing role of UKHSA"
  ),
  Col6 = c(
    "establish practice consensus and reporting guidelines for evaluation metrics",
    "establish ethical standards and include evaluation in modeller training",
    "fit for purpose; a role model for other model-to-policy settings"
  ),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

ft <- flextable(df_main)

# Set header labels for the first header row
ft <- set_header_labels(ft,
  Col1 = "",
  Col2 = "",
  Col3 = "Data synthesis",
  Col4 = "Evidence synthesis",
  Col5 = "Saturation/degradation?",
  Col6 = "Outcomes"
)

# Add a second header row
ft <- add_header_lines(ft, values = "")

# Merge cells in header row 2 for "Quantity of epidemiological intelligence" across columns 3-5
ft <- merge_at(ft, i = 2, j = 3:5, part = "header")

# Body merges: Quality spans rows 1-2 in column 1
ft <- merge_at(ft, i = 1:2, j = 1, part = "body")

# Case study spans columns 1-2 in row 3
ft <- merge_at(ft, i = 3, j = 1:2, part = "body")

ft <- autofit(ft)
ft
```
